item.title: TG Jones wins court approval to close up to 150 stores
item.description: Proposal follows buyout owner’s admission that WHSmith’s former high street business is ‘almost completely broken’

item.title: FT Financial Literacy and Inclusion Campaign
item.description: The FT invites readers to join our campaign to promote financial literacy in the UK and around the world

item.title: BlueCrest warns UK is bad for business after losing £200mn tax battle
item.description: Investment firm says the country is ‘no longer a serious contender’

item.title: ABF sugar profits hit by Strait of Hormuz closure
item.description: Primark sales decline as FTSE 100 group struggles ahead of planned split of retail and food businesses

item.title: Getty scraps $3.7bn Shutterstock merger after UK watchdog order
item.description: US regulators had already cleared combination of rivals when CMA imposed conditions

item.title: UK watchdog launches in-depth probe into Virgin Media O2 owners’ £2bn deal
item.description: Review of Telefónica and Liberty Global’s planned takeover of Netomnia could delay consolidation in fibre broadband sector

item.title: UK house price inflation rises as falling energy costs soften interest rate expectations
item.description: Annual rate of inflation rose to 2.2% in June, up from 1.7% in May, according to Nationwide

item.title: CEOs beware — here comes the algo tone police
item.description: What is the appropriate amount of arousal for an earnings call?

item.title: The job market is reaccelerating. Is the economy?
item.description: Plus the bitcoin clown car company crash

item.title: European armies already filling holes made by US withdrawals, says Rutte
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Ireland sets out its priorities as it assumes the EU presidency

item.title: FirstFT: Europe defence drive benefits US jobs, Rutte says
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Made-in-Germany US weapons and French-US logistics deal

item.title: Introducing: The Story of Money
item.description: A new video podcast from The FT exploring the history of finance

item.title: Wall Street’s $60tn nepo baby boom
item.description: The great wealth transfer is disrupting the business of managing money for rich people

item.title: CMA CGM nears $1.4bn deal for FedEx logistics unit
item.description: French shipping group aims to expand in US and strengthen foothold in supply chain

item.title: Germany woos Trump with plan to make US weapons in Europe
item.description: Talks on ‘joint production concepts’ accelerate ahead of Nato summit

item.title: Small fraction of EU crypto groups hold licence as new rules come into force
item.description: Stringent bloc-wide MiCA regulations push unlicensed digital-assets companies to offload customers

item.title: Planes leaving half full in EU border chaos, says industry
item.description: Airports and airlines warn delays will worsen over summer unless they are allowed to abandon new checks

item.title: US fossil fuel power spending to beat China for the first time in decades
item.description: Boom in gas turbines to meet demand from data centres, IEA figures show

item.title: The town that Elon Musk built
item.description: A new city of SpaceX workers is emerging out of the Texas sand, complete with Trump’s border patrols, a vast natural gas project and the fruits of the AI boom

item.title: KKR plots entry into UK and European pension buyouts
item.description: Private capital rivals have piled into lucrative pension risk-transfer business

item.title: Should I buy a leasehold flat or wait?
item.description: I’m worried about what might happen when the government bans the leasehold system

item.title: The Swiss are ‘sole-searching’ about a shoe
item.description: Debate over the On sportswear company raises questions about what should be labelled as a national product

item.title: NatWest sued for £250mn by firm linked to Thurrock council scandal
item.description: Rockfire Group’s liquidators claim lender’s Royal Bank of Scotland subsidiary processed unauthorised payments

item.title: Why Britain’s electricity prices risk remaining high
item.description: Analysts argue that household bills will rise as lower wholesale costs are offset by higher spending on electricity networks and renewables subsidies

item.title: AI jitters give Indian IPOs a moment to shine
item.description: Two Indian listings this year are expected to raise a combined $7bn

item.title: Uniqlo’s plan to dominate global fashion
item.description: The clothing retailer forged in Japan’s post-bubble economy is pinning its hopes on Europe and North America

item.title: ‘We have to kill diesel’: China’s e-truck rival to Tesla seeks $2bn US Spac deal
item.description: Windrose Electric chief says US tariffs are not high enough to keep it out of America

item.title: Chanel’s ‘Blazy mania’ raises pressure on Dior in luxury’s zero-sum game
item.description: The two French houses have appointed star designers to revive sales in tough market

item.title: The 1980s Garfield buyout that changed corporate finance
item.description: It was a conservative Catholic from the Nixon administration who launched the private equity industry

item.title: Why Indonesians are souring on Prabowo
item.description: Students are protesting as the country’s currency weakens
